summary: UFC 330 (Aug 15, 2026, Philadelphia) is a routine championship PPV, not a Trump-hosted special event. Trump attends UFC events frequently but not all of them. Proximity to DC, the swing-state venue, a championship main event, and his close Dana White friendship push the probability modestly above the status-quo NO. Offsetting these: no announcement of attendance, having just hosted the massive White House event two months earlier, a non-Trump-favored main event (Makhachev vs Garry), and post-terror-plot security considerations. Net estimate ~27%. A confirmed attendance announcement would sharply raise this; the forecast sits near the base rate because the positive and negative pulls roughly cancel.

factors.description: Base rate: Trump attends only a subset of numbered UFC PPVs per year, giving unconditional per-event attendance around 25%
Philadelphia is close to DC in swing-state PA he won; championship main event; close Dana White friendship and established UFC attendance pattern
Just hosted the massive UFC Freedom 250 White House event two months prior; may have satiated near-term UFC appetite
No specific announcement that Trump will attend; status quo is NO
Main event lacks Trump-allied American fighters he tends to show up for; security concerns post-thwarted WH terror plot

reasoning:
**Forecast: 27%**

UFC 330 is a routine numbered event (Makhachev vs. Machado Garry) at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia on August 15, 2026 — not the special White House card (UFC Freedom 250) that Trump hosted on June 14, 2026. While Trump attends UFC events frequently and is close with Dana White, and Philadelphia is a convenient swing-state venue near DC, there's no announcement of his attendance, he just hosted the massive White House event two months prior, and the main event lacks the Trump-allied fighters he typically shows up for. This lands modestly above the status-quo NO at **0.27**.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as Yes if President Donald Trump is anywhere within the grounds of the venue where [UFC 330](https://www.ufc.com/event/ufc-330) takes place. Otherwise this resolves as No, including if the event does not take place before September 1, 2026.

factors.description: Base rate: Trump attends only a subset of numbered UFC PPVs per year (a few of ~13), giving an unconditional per-event attendance around 25%
Philadelphia is close to DC (short flight) in the swing state of PA he won; championship main event; close friendship with Dana White and established pattern of UFC attendance
Just hosted the massive UFC Freedom 250 White House event two months prior (June 2026); may have satiated near-term UFC appetite
No specific announcement that Trump will attend; status quo is NO and events like this often pass without presidential attendance
Main event (Makhachev, Russian, vs Garry, Irish) lacks the American/Trump-allied fighters he tends to show up for; security concerns post-thwarted terror plot at WH event

input.anchor: ~25% base rate for Trump attending a given numbered UFC PPV during his presidency (attends a handful of ~13 per year)
input.assessment: UFC 330 is a routine numbered event in Philadelphia, not a special Trump-hosted event. Trump attends UFC events frequently but not all of them. Proximity to DC and his Dana White friendship push probability up, but absence of any announcement, having just hosted the White House event, and a non-Trump-favored main event push it down. Net estimate ~27%, modestly above the status-quo NO but well below even odds. A specific announcement of attendance would sharply raise this.
input.tool_audit: web_search returned solid confirmation of UFC 330 date/location (Aug 15, 2026, Philadelphia) and Trump's strong UFC attendance pattern including the June 2026 White House event. No CP data available (AIB). No tool failures.
input.process_reflection: Straightforward question; web search sufficient. Would have benefited from a quick structured list of every UFC numbered event Trump has personally attended to nail down the base rate, but qualitative pattern was clear enough.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
I verified each factor against the research trace and found the evidence chain sound.

GROUNDING CHECK (all factors supported):
- Factor 1 (base rate ~25%): The specific "a few of ~13 per year" figure is not backed by an enumerated source in the trace — the agent did not extract a list of Trump's actual UFC attendances. However, the core conclusion (Trump attends some but not all numbered PPVs) is grounded in the trace (WH event, UFC 314 Miami, Dana White friendship). This is a soft anchor, not a hallucination — the thing that moves the forecast (an unconditional attendance rate well below 50%) is reasonable and defensible.
- Factor 2 (proximity/friendship): Dana White friendship confirmed (ESPN timeline); proximity/championship framing is reasonable inference. Grounded.
- Factor 3 (just hosted WH event): UFC Freedom 250, June 14 2026, Trump cage-side — directly confirmed in trace. Grounded.
- Factor 4 (no announcement): Properly an absence-of-evidence factor; no source announcing attendance was found. Grounded.
- Factor 5 (non-American main event + security): Makhachev (Russia) vs Garry (Ireland) confirmed; FBI-thwarted terror plot after WH event confirmed. Logit sign (negative) matches description. Grounded.

NO DOUBLE-COUNTING: Each factor draws on distinct evidence (base rate vs. proximity vs. satiation vs. announcement vs. matchup/security).

RESOLUTION CRITERIA: Provided and clear ("within the grounds of the venue"); factors and assessment engage with them properly. Not MISSING, so no recovery needed.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: The WH event (June 14) precedes publication (June 29), but the agent correctly uses it only as base-rate context and a mild satiation negative — NOT as resolving the question. UFC 330 is a future event (Aug 15). No Case 2 violation.

TOOL AUDIT: Agent claims "no tool failures" — matches ground truth (0 errors). "No CP data (AIB)" is consistent. The 4 wikipedia calls aren't narrated but their content surfaced in the web_search api_data extracts; no contradiction.

COUNTERARGUMENT: Genuinely strong — builds a coherent alternative story (near-routine attendance at marquee east-coast cards, swing state, short flight) and names the specific trigger (an announcement) that would flip the forecast. Not a token gesture.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate lands in the ~22-32% range. The positive proximity/friendship factors roughly offset the satiation/no-announcement/security negatives, leaving the forecast near the base rate. The agent's 27% (just 2pp above its 25% anchor) is well-calibrated, and confidence of 0.60 appropriately reflects the soft base rate and the absence of any attendance announcement. No meaningful divergence; no anchor-departure concern.

WORLDVIEW: No related Trump-UFC entries exist; no cross-question or binary/numeric consistency issues.

Only minor note: the "~13 PPVs" base-rate denominator is an estimate rather than a sourced count, but it does not change the forecast direction or materially affect the probability.
